differentials.raw: Involvement of two / more parts of CNS

differentials.name: ≥ 2 episodes lasting > 24 hr less than 1 month apart
differentials.raw: ≥ 2 episodes lasting > 24 hr less than 1 month apart

differentials.name: Slow stepwise progression of signs + symptoms
differentials.raw: Slow stepwise progression of signs + symptoms

differentials.name: Rudick red flags (suggests diagnosis other than MS):
differentials.raw: Rudick red flags (suggests diagnosis other than MS):

differentials.name: number + extent of plaques correlate with degree of disability
differentials.raw: number + extent of plaques correlate with degree of disability

differentials.name: Cord tumor
differentials.raw: Cord tumor (follow-up after 6 weeks without decrease in size of lesion)

differentials.name: Postviral syndromes Rx:   steroids
differentials.raw: Postviral syndromes Rx:   steroids (incite rapid decrease in size of lesions with loss of enhancement)

differentials.name: Acute disseminated encephalomyelitis
differentials.raw: Acute disseminated encephalomyelitis (ADEM), subacute sclerosing panencephalitis (lesions of similar age)

differentials.name: Susac syndrome
differentials.raw: Susac syndrome (encephalopathy + branch retinal artery occlusion + hearing loss)

differentials.name: Small vessel ischemia
differentials.raw: Small vessel ischemia (patients > 50 years of age, lesions < 5 mm, NOT infratentorial)

differentials.name: AIDS, CNS vasculitis, migraine, radiation injury, lymphoma, sarcoidosis, tuberculosis, systemic lupus erythematosus, cysticercosis, metastases, multifocal glioma, neurofibromatosis, contusions
differentials.raw: AIDS, CNS vasculitis, migraine, radiation injury, lymphoma, sarcoidosis, tuberculosis, systemic lupus erythematosus, cysticercosis, metastases, multifocal glioma, neurofibromatosis, contusions

context: Location: subependymal periventricular location (along lateral aspects of atria + occipital horns), corpus callosum, internal capsule, centrum semiovale, corona radiata, optic nerves, chiasm, optic tract, brainstem (ventrolateral aspect of pons at 5th nerve root entry), cerebellar peduncles (CLASSIC), cerebellum; rather symmetric involvement of cerebral hemispheres; subcortical U fibers NOT spared ◊  10% of MS lesions occur in gray matter! ◊  Number + extent of plaques correlate with duration of disease + degree of cognitive impairment √  lesion size of 1-25 (majority between 5 and 10) mm: √
